title: T-013 — Quality hold gate service (D-SHP-13 soft + hard gate) shared across pick/pack/ship (11-shipping-c)
prompt:
# T-013 — Quality hold gate service (D-SHP-13)

PRD: docs/prd/11-SHIPPING-PRD.md §6 D-SHP-13 Quality Hold Soft Gate, §10 Quality Hold Integration, §11 V-SHIP-PICK-02, V-SHIP-PICK-03, V-SHIP-SHIP-03
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Shared TypeScript service `shipping-quality-hold-gate.ts` consumed by pick, pack and ship-confirm Server Actions (T-007, T-016, T-020) that determines whether a given LP can proceed and returns `{ ok, severity, hold_details, blocker_code }`. Single source of truth for D-SHP-13 logic.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/web/lib/services/shipping-quality-hold-gate.ts` exports:
   - `evaluateLpForShipping(lpId): Promise<HoldGateDecision>` — queries `v_active_holds` (09-QA T-010) for `reference_type='lp' AND reference_id=lpId AND status IN ('open','investigating')`; returns highest-severity decision.
   - Decision shape: `{ ok: boolean, severity: 'critical'|'major'|'medium'|'minor'|null, hold_details: HoldRow[], blocker_code?: string, requires_override?: { permission, reason_required: boolean } }`.
2. Severity mapping per §10.1:
   - `critical` → `ok=false`, blocker_code='qa.critical_hold' (HARD BLOCK).
   - `major` → `ok=true`, requires_override={ permission: 'ship.alloc.override', reason_required: true }, role hint 'shipping_qa'.
   - `medium` → `ok=true`, requires_override={ permission: 'ship.alloc.override', reason_required: true }, role hint 'shipping_manager'.
   - `minor` → `ok=true`, requires_override={ permission: 'ship.pick.execute', reason_required: true }, operator confirm only.
3. Service consumed by:
   - `confirmSO` (T-007) — runs `evaluateLpForShipping` for every allocated LP; if any critical → block.
   - `pick.execute` (T-016) — V-SHIP-PICK-02 (soft warn) and V-SHIP-PICK-03 (hard block on critical).
   - `ship.confirm` (T-020) — V-SHIP-SHIP-03 (no critical holds on shipment LPs).
4. Service emits a `shipping.quality_hold.overridden` event (consumer 09-QA T-011) on every confirmed override decision, with `audit_hash`, severity, hold_id, user_id, reason_code, ts.
5. P1 implementation does NOT activate `batch_release_gate_v1` DSL (Phase 2 per §10.2). Document the P2 evolution path in code comments.
6. Cache TTL 30s using @monopilot/runtime-cache (Vercel runtime cache, T-118-adjacent) keyed by `lp_id` to reduce v_active_holds query pressure — invalidated on `quality.hold.created|released|severity_changed` event consume (T-118 OTLP/observability is the consumer base).

## Files
- apps/web/lib/services/shipping-quality-hold-gate.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/services/__tests__/shipping-quality-hold-gate.test.ts [create]
- packages/events/src/shipping.ts [modify — ShippingQualityHoldOverridden]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given an LP with active hold.severity='critical', when evaluateLpForShipping runs, then it returns { ok: false, severity: 'critical', blocker_code: 'qa.critical_hold' }.
2. Given an LP with active hold.severity='major', when evaluateLpForShipping runs, then it returns { ok: true, severity: 'major', requires_override: { permission: 'ship.alloc.override', reason_required: true } }.
3. Given an LP with no active hold, when evaluateLpForShipping runs, then it returns { ok: true, severity: null, requires_override: undefined }.
4. Given a service call writes an override decision, when it succeeds, then a shipping.quality_hold.overridden outbox event is enqueued with audit_hash and severity field populated.
5. Given a `quality.hold.severity_changed` event is consumed for an LP, when the cache is checked, then the cached decision for that lp_id is invalidated and re-queried.
6. Given grep `current_setting\('app\.(tenant_id|current_org_id)'\)` on the service file, when run, then zero matches (depends on calling Server Action's withOrgContext wrap).

## Test strategy
- RED: vitest with testcontainers Postgres seeding quality_holds + v_active_holds view; asserts AC 1-6 + cache invalidation behavior.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run shipping-quality-hold-gate.test.ts`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not query `quality_holds` directly — consume v_active_holds (09-QA T-010 contract).
- Do not silently override critical holds (D-SHP-13 HARD BLOCK).
- Do not skip emitting `shipping.quality_hold.overridden` event — 09-QA audit consumer (T-011) depends on it.
- Do not implement `batch_release_gate_v1` DSL here (Phase 2).
- Do not cache for more than 30s — staleness compromises HARD BLOCK semantic.
- Do not read GUC settings directly.

pipeline_inputs.description: Shared quality-hold gate service (D-SHP-13 soft + hard gate) consumed by pick/pack/ship; emits shipping.quality_hold.overridden audit event.
pipeline_inputs.details: 30s cache via runtime-cache, invalidated on 09-QA event consume. Severity mapping per §10.1 table. P1 does NOT activate batch_release_gate_v1 DSL (Phase 2 per §10.2).
out_of_scope: Do not duplicate v_active_holds in shipping schema.
cross_module_dependencies: 09-quality/T-064 (consume gate — evaluateLpForShipping is the holdsGuard implementation; shipping depends on this gate per 2026-05-14 audit)
acceptance_criteria: Given an LP with critical hold, when evaluateLpForShipping (09-quality T-064 gate) runs, then returns { ok: false, severity: 'critical', holdId }; AND given major hold, returns { ok: true, severity: 'major', holdId } (warn only); AND given no active hold, returns { ok: true, severity: null }.

Given a service call writes an override decision, when it succeeds, then shipping.quality_hold.overridden outbox event is emitted with override details; AND given quality.hold.severity_changed event consumed for a cached LP, then cache is invalidated within 30s.
Given grep current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id') on the service file, when run, then zero matches.
Given two concurrent evaluateLpForShipping calls for the same LP, when run, then consistent result is returned and no race condition in cache invalidation (cache TTL ≤30s per BRCGS audit readiness).
test_strategy: RED: vitest with testcontainers Postgres + quality_holds + v_active_holds seeded fixtures asserting AC 1-6.
risk_red_lines: Do not query quality_holds directly — consume v_active_holds.
Do not silently override critical holds.
Do not cache for more than 30s.
Do not bypass the 09-quality consume gate (T-064 v_active_holds) — every consume path must call holdsGuard(lpId, lotId) before mutating consumption tables.
